front: Work: in this novel, Jose Arcadio Buendia founds a town, goes mad babbling in Latin, and is tied to a chestnut tree
back: One Hundred Years of Solitude (Gabriel Garcia Marquez)

front: Work: in this novel, Ursula fears incest will produce a child with a pig's tail; the last descendant is born with one and eaten alive by ants
back: One Hundred Years of Solitude (Gabriel Garcia Marquez)

front: Work: in this novel, a beautiful woman ascends into the sky while folding laundry/sheets
back: One Hundred Years of Solitude (Gabriel Garcia Marquez) — Remedios the Beauty

front: Work: in this novel, Melquiades visits with trinkets and ice; his manuscript predicts the town's destruction
back: One Hundred Years of Solitude (Gabriel Garcia Marquez)

front: Work: in this novel, a colonel starts 32 failed uprisings, fathers 17 identically-named sons, and endlessly makes and melts miniature golden fish
back: One Hundred Years of Solitude (Gabriel Garcia Marquez) — Colonel Aureliano Buendia

front: Work: in this novel, Mauricio Babilonia is surrounded by yellow butterflies (inspired by the author's grandmother)
back: One Hundred Years of Solitude (Gabriel Garcia Marquez)

front: Work: the author smoked 60 cigarettes a day in a Mexico City apartment while writing this novel, where he read Pedro Paramo
back: One Hundred Years of Solitude (Gabriel Garcia Marquez)

front: Work: in this story, a girl transformed into a tarantula for disobedience takes customers away from a mute old man imprisoned in a chicken coop
back: "A Very Old Man with Enormous Wings" (Gabriel Garcia Marquez)
work: "A Very Old Man with Enormous Wings"

front: Work: Dr. Juvenal Urbino smells bitter almonds from a gold cyanide suicide, then dies falling from a ladder retrieving his parrot from a mango tree
back: Love in the Time of Cholera (Gabriel Garcia Marquez)
work: Love in the Time of Cholera

front: Work: in this novella, the Vicario twins stab Santiago Nasar to avenge their sister Angela, who was returned on her wedding night
back: Chronicle of a Death Foretold (Gabriel Garcia Marquez)

front: Work: this dictator novel inspired by Franco features a 200-year-old General who hears sad whistling from his massive testicle and is eaten by vultures
back: The Autumn of the Patriarch (Gabriel Garcia Marquez)

front: Work: in this story, villagers make the memory of a beautiful corpse named Esteban 'eternal' by painting their houses in bright colors
back: "The Handsomest Drowned Man in the World" (Gabriel Garcia Marquez)
work: "The Handsomest Drowned Man in the World"

front: Work: in this novella, the title character considers selling a rooster trained for cockfighting while waiting for his pension
back: No One Writes to the Colonel (Gabriel Garcia Marquez)

front: Work: in this novella, the title character is forced to become a prostitute by her grandmother; Ulises helps her escape with diamonds hidden in oranges
Work: this Nobel speech quotes Faulkner ('I decline to accept the end of man'), opens with Pigafetta's account, and refuses to embody Tonio Kroger's dream
back: "The Solitude of Latin America" (Gabriel Garcia Marquez, 1982 Nobel speech)
work: Nobel Speech / "The Solitude of Latin America"

front: Work: 14 newspaper articles about the sole survivor of a ship that sank from contraband; forced the closure of El Espectador by Rojas Pinilla
back: The Story of a Shipwrecked Sailor (Gabriel Garcia Marquez)

front: Author: in 1976, this author was punched in the face by Mario Vargas Llosa at a Mexico City movie premiere ('That's for what you did to Patricia')
Author: Juan Rulfo's Pedro Paramo deeply influenced this author, who memorized much of it and bought dozens of copies for friends
Author: Reinaldo Arenas asked whether this author was 'a henchman or an ass' for his friendship with Fidel Castro
